items.title: SA's largest electorate to be renamed in honour of Aboriginal leader

children.content: South Australia’s largest federal electorate will be renamed in honour of an Aboriginal leader. Experts say it is also the state’s most likely to fall to One Nation.

gallery.altText: a green voting slip is dropped into a voting box

thumbnail.altText: a green voting slip is dropped into a voting box

items.title: 'Like The Castle': Restaurant owners launch petition against land grab

children.content: The owners of a Mount Barker restaurant have launched a petition to block the government's proposed acquisition of the site on which their business stands.

image.altText: A man stands in front of a artistically graffitied wall with his arms crossed.

items.title: Crows, Darcy Fogarty challenge three-match striking ban at tribunal

children.content: The Crows challenge Darcy Fogarty's three-match striking ban at the AFL Tribunal, as they try to avoid losing a key forward for vital games in the lead-up to finals. Follow live.

image.altText: An Adelaide Crows AFL forward points ahead in celebration after kicking a goal during a game.

items.title: Truck driver reported after concrete slabs fell on major SA road

children.content: A truck driver has been reported over a multi-car crash in which concrete slabs fell onto the North-South Motorway, in Adelaide's north.

image.altText: Large concrete slabs fall from a moving truck on a motorway

items.title: Special guest dazzles aspiring astronauts at Space Day Out

children.content: Young South Australians got one step closer to achieving their space exploration dreams on Sunday when they met a "real-life" astronaut.

image.altText: A lady in a blue astronaut suit looks at the camera with a smile

items.title: 'Right fit': Ken Hinkley announced as inaugural Tasmania Devils coach

children.content: The former Port Adelaide coach is formally announced as the inaugural coach of the Tasmania Devils. It completes a year-long hunt for a senior coach for the AFL's newest club.

gallery.altText: A bald middle aged man in a dark polo looks into the distance

thumbnail.altText: A bald middle aged man in a dark polo looks into the distance

items.title: For some in Australia, hope is fading and anger is rising

children.content: Sulaiman Zandinan says Australia is amazing. Wendy Newman says people in the bush are "not country bumpkins". The ABC is inviting you to have your say about the issues, challenges and opportunities that shape your life in 2026.

image.altText: A man with curly hair smiles while holding a sports whistle looking away

items.title: 'Almost certainly new' species of giant earthworms found in SA

children.content: A South Australian ecologist has been tracking giant earthworm sightings for 30 years, despite the species not officially existing in the state.

image.altText: A child holding up a worm which stretches from above his head to his waist

items.title: Once known as Little Beirut, stories of Adelaide's historical corner uncovered

children.content: It's been a three year labour of love probing into the past and building on the fragments of a hidden history to write Logan St and get it to the stage.

gallery.altText: Alexis West and Ali Ammouchi sit on stage in rehearsals for Logan St.

thumbnail.altText: Alexis West and Ali Ammouchi sit on stage in rehearsals for Logan St.

items.title: New energy scheme adding to bills, SA government admits

children.content: A new SA government energy scheme set to collect almost $44 million from electricity users this financial year has been described by a critic as a "hidden charge".

image.altText: A Stobie pole with powerlines.

items.title: Uranium export deal sparks talk of opportunities for Broken Hill

children.content: Australia's trade deal to supply India with unenriched uranium sparks conversations about potential new mining opportunities in Broken Hill.

image.altText: An aerial view of a mine in remote Australia.

items.title: From 40 cartographers to two, street directories are nearing end of the road

children.content: While sales of the once-ubiquitous street directories have fallen dramatically since their peak in the 1980s, they still have a niche market.

image.altText: A street directory sits on a steering wheel

items.title: More than 20 SA Department for Child Protection employees stood down

children.content: At the end of February, South Australia's Department for Child Protection had 12 open investigations into staff as a result of a care concern, Freedom of Information documents show.

image.altText: Empty swings in a park with a pram parked in the background.

items.title: Three years after fatal shark attack, community cements teacher's legacy

children.content: A regional community was left shaken after Simon Baccanello was killed in a shark attack in 2023. Now, his legacy will be cemented through a new sporting complex.

image.altText: A smiling man wearing a light blue shirt with his arms down and hands together

items.title: Wok the Duck owners seething after compulsory acquisition shock

children.content: Aston Hills Mount Barker developers and a popular restaurant say their investments have been turned upside down by a state government compulsory acquisition announced this week.

image.altText: Three people stand in a restaurant with a sign behind them saying 'boom'

items.title: US launches more strikes on Iran and IRGC attacks oil tankers

children.content: The US launches a new round of air strikes on Iran after announcing the death of another American service member, and the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps says it has immobilised two oil tankers in the Strait of Hormuz.

image.altText: US soldiers bathed in orange light, load missiles during the latest wave of strikes

items.title: Alone, robbed and in pain: A man finds himself on Sydney's streets

children.content: People with lived experience of homelessness say the issue is deepening as housing stock remains out of reach and community providers are left struggling to cope with demand.

items.title: Premier says 'witch' billboard dehumanises women, drives violence

items.title: Why the Tate brothers were arrested and what they are charged with

children.content: Influencer brothers Andrew and Tristan Tate have been arrested in Miami after UK prosecutors brought further rape and sex trafficking charges against them. Here's what we know about the arrests and extradition request.

image.altText: Andrew Tate, left, watches his brother Tristan speak.

items.title: Lesbian couple told they are no longer the same family by fertility clinic

children.content: Queensland Health advice, given to fertility providers to help navigate the state's assisted reproductive technology legislation, has had a "devastating" impact on some same-sex couples trying to have more children.

image.altText: Two women kissing the cheeks of a young girl at a kitchen table.

items.title: Australia is making humanoid robots in the midst of its AI reckoning

children.content: Australia this week made an important step forward in grappling with the AI revolution. Meanwhile, in Melbourne a company is making humanoid robots.

items.title: War and AI worries drag down Wall Street, ASX edges higher, oil jumps

items.title: Home Affairs asks DV survivors to prove abuser relationship 'genuine'

children.content: A federal government safety net has been identified as one of the biggest stressors for migrant women experiencing domestic and family violence.

items.title: Asylum seekers Australia sent to Nauru say they are going hungry

children.content: According to a survey of 78 asylum seekers on Nauru, 99 per cent are regularly skipping meals because they cannot not afford to buy enough food, while more than three-quarters rarely have access to free, clean drinking water.

image.altText: Refugees and asylum seekers wander around the detention centre on Nauru

items.title: Holocaust museum staff and volunteers targeted by hateful comments

children.content: Melbourne Holocaust Museum has had to step up security since 2020 following a rise in incidents that have seen threatening voice messages and hateful comments left online.

items.title: Pauline Hanson 'not backing away' from controversial far-right interview

children.content: The One Nation leader was a guest on the podcast of controversial far-right UK figure Tommy Robinson, where she lamented the end of the White Australia policy.

items.title: Rules to tighten on AI use in decision-making by government departments

children.content: Agencies like Centrelink and Services Australia will have to follow a framework to ensure decision-making is "fair and accurate" if they use AI.
